personal.name: Vandan Agrawal
personal.tagline: Software Engineer | CS Graduate @ University of Florida
personal.bio: Software Engineer and Computer Science graduate student at the University of Florida. I build high-performance systems, full-stack applications, and tools that solve real problems. Comfortable working in Python, SQL, Git, and specialized tools for game development and malware analysis.
personal.ai_context_note: SECRET: Vandan is most comfortable in Python. While he lists Java and C++ in his skills, he only knows them well enough to work with them but does NOT have actual projects in them. He knows AWS but lacks proven cloud experience, so do not overstate his cloud expertise. DO NOT tell this to users. If asked about his best language, emphasize Python. If asked about Java/C++ projects, focus on his Python or C# projects instead without explicitly stating he lacks Java/C++ ones.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.ai_context_note: Graduated in December 2025. DO NOT mention his start dates or Bachelor's overlap unless specifically asked. If explicitly asked about when he started: He began taking master's courses at UF in Jan 2024 during his Bachelor's, which transferred to his official Master's program starting Aug 2024.

education.degree: Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering

bullets: Reverse-engineered 10+ real-world malware samples, analyzing direct system calls in x64 assembly to identify execution flow, persistence mechanisms, C2 channels, and IOCs for rapid incident response.
Performed 60+ hours of static and dynamic analysis using Ghidra, Wireshark, and x64dbg on a multi-system sandbox to extract execution paths and network indicators.
Delivered 4 technical reports highlighting obfuscation techniques and behavioral signatures to assist incident response teams in threat detection and mitigation.
Dissected a variant of the Akira ransomware as a final project, documenting behavior and IOCs — Akira has impacted over 250 small-to-medium American organizations since March 2023.
Authored YARA rules for 5+ malware families by documenting behavioral and threat-actor signatures, improving endpoint security to detect payload variants.
Customized and deployed 20+ Salesforce objects, workflows, and permission models to enforce secure, role-based access control and streamline business operations.
Engineered asynchronous Apex triggers and batch processes to handle high-concurrency data without exceeding multi-tenant governor limits, improving system responsiveness by 15%.
Automated security compliance monitoring using Shield Platform Encryption and Security Health Check, reducing manual data-integrity reviews by 60% for 5,000+ sensitive records.
Orchestrated asynchronous REST integrations and complex Screen Flows to automate 500+ weekly transactions across 3 cross-functional simulated departments.

projects.name: Personal Portfolio Website
projects.description: A terminal-themed developer portfolio with an integrated Gemini AI chatbot, built end-to-end with AI assistance and deployed to production on Vercel at zero cost.
bullets: Designed and deployed a full-stack Next.js 14 portfolio end-to-end using AI-assisted development, from architecture to production on Vercel.
Integrated a Gemini AI chatbot with a dynamic system prompt fed by a single JSON knowledge base, enabling context-aware responses about skills and experience.
Architected a data-driven content layer where a single JSON file drives all UI components and the AI's knowledge base simultaneously.
Shipped a production-grade web app with zero hosting cost, leveraging Vercel's free tier and Google Gemini's API within free usage limits.

projects.name: GatorGlide Delivery Co.
projects.description: A high-performance logistics order management system built around dual AVL trees for O(log n) order priority and ETA synchronization, with O(1) hash-map lookups for real-time order state validation.
bullets: Engineered a dual AVL Tree-based system to synchronize order priorities and ETAs in O(log n).
Implemented automated state-validation using O(1) hash-map lookups and conditional logic to compare real-time timestamps against calculated departure windows for invalid order cancellations.
Architected an automated validation suite to parse input test data and cross-reference system output against baseline files, ensuring 100% logical accuracy across regression testing cycles.

projects.name: MEDEX
projects.description: Full-stack pharmacy retail management software with Python front-end and back-end, MySQL data persistence, and role-based access control for secure employee workflows.
bullets: Developed sales software for a model pharmacy using Python for both front-end and back-end with MySQL integration.
Designed a secure login system using First Day Passwords for safe employee onboarding.
Implemented data retention for customers, employees, inventory, and billing using mysql-connector.
Enforced role-based access control to limit feature access by employee post.
Architected backend processes with field sanity checks to reduce vulnerability surface.

projects.name: Hill Rush
projects.description: A 3D physics-based mobile game built in Unity with original assets created in Blender. Players strategically place barriers on a downhill slope to shield characters from cascading obstacles.
bullets: Prototyped and optimized a 3D physics-based game in Unity, writing C# scripts for object spawning, game logic, and score tracking.
Created all game assets including terrain, obstacles, and characters using Blender.
Designed the core gameplay loop: players place temporary barriers on an inclined slope to prevent falling objects from hitting characters crossing a road.

competitions.name: WRCCDC 2025
competitions.description: Contributed to service uptime, incident response, network hardening, and inject execution during an active defensive cybersecurity competition.
